feedback.text: The Digital Fairness Act should have a look at digital ownership. Today, consumers buy digital goods like games, software, music, or movies, but often lose access later due to platform shutdowns or revoked licenses. This would be unacceptable with physical goods. If you buy a DVD or a Hammer, you own it and can use it as long as it works. Digital products should be no different. The method of distribution may have changed, but the principle of ownership must remain. Consumers deserve lasting access to the digital content they legally purchase and sellers should be held accountable to ensure that their digital services are REASONABLY usable after platform shutdowns.
